channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Bolivian prosecutors seek the arrest of former President Morales in sexual abuse case
item.description: Prosecutors in Bolivia are seeking the arrest of former President Evo Morales in connection to a sexual abuse investigation involving his alleged fathering of a child with a 15-year-old girl.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Negotiators say Israel and Hamas are inching toward a ceasefire deal. This is what it may look like
item.description: After months of deadlock, Israel and Hamas appear to be moving closer toward a ceasefire to end their 14-month war.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says Turkey ‘did an unfriendly takeover’ in Syria as US-brokered cease-fire appears to fail
item.description: President-elect Trump described the fall of the Assad regime in Syria as an "unfriendly takeover" by neighboring Turkey as sources tell Fox News Digital the U.S.-brokered cease-fire has collapsed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: France rushes aid to Mayotte after Cyclone Chido leaves hundreds feared dead
item.description: MAMOUDZOU, Mayotte (AP) — France used ships and military aircraft to rush rescuers and supplies to Mayotte on Monday after the tiny French island territory off Africa was battered by its worst cyclone in nearly a century. Authorities fear hundreds and possibly thousands of people have died.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, THOMAS ADAMSON and RAINAT ALILOIFFA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Palestinian security forces launch a rare crackdown on militants in the West Bank
item.description: Palestinian security forces have launched a rare crackdown against local militant groups in the northern West Bank.
item.dc:creator: Majdi Mohammed And Aref Tufaha, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Canadian finance minister notes Trump tariff threat in resignation letter
item.description: Canada's finance minister noted President-elect Trump’s tariff threat in her resignation letter dated Monday. “Our country today faces a grave challenge,” Chrystia Freeland said in a letter addressed to Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: UN announces initiative to overcome political deadlock in Libya
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The top U.N. official in Libya announced a new initiative Monday to overcome a three-year deadlock and move the divided oil-rich North African nation toward a national election. The U.N. deputy special envoy for Libya, Stephanie Koury, told the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to say ‘Merry Christmas’ in 10 languages to friends around the world
item.description: This guide will pique your curiosity with how to say "Merry Christmas" in different languages; plus, how Christmas is celebrated in various countries around the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says Russia-Ukraine peace may be harder than Middle East
item.description: President-elect Trump on Monday expressed confidence that he could settle the war between Russia and Ukraine but also stressed that it would be difficult and possibly harder to solve than the conflict between Israel and the Palestinian militant group Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Russian presence in Syria remains. But it's unclear for how long
item.description: Russia’s intervention on behalf of former Syrian leader Bashar Assad once turned the tide of Syria's civil war.
item.dc:creator: Abby Sewell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Syria's ousted leader Assad says he wanted to keep fighting but Russian allies evacuated him
item.description: DAMASCUS (AP) — Ousted Syrian leader Bashar Assad said Monday he wanted to stay in the country after rebels captured the capital, but the Russian military evacuated him from their base in western Syria after it came under attack.
item.dc:creator: SARAH EL DEEB and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuelan rights group reports 3rd in-custody death among people detained in post-election unrest
item.description: A man detained in Venezuela during the civil unrest that followed the country’s disputed July presidential election has died while in custody.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine details North Korean casualties in Russia's Kursk region
item.description: Ukraine is reporting at least 30 North Korean soldiers were killed or wounded over the weekend around several villages on the front in Russia's Kursk region.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: Assad paints himself as 'custodian' to Syria as picture unfolds on collapse of Damascus
item.description: Former Syrian President Bashar al-Assad broke his silence since fleeing the nation following the Islamist rebel takeover and looked to portray himself only ever as a "custodian" of Syria.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McConnell urges Trump to reject isolationism
item.description: Outgoing Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) is calling on President-elect Trump to reject isolationists in the Republican Party who will argue for the United States to focus on China at the expense of Ukraine and other areas of the world.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Assad in first public comments since ouster says he wanted to keep fighting in Syria
item.description: Overthrown Syrian leader Bashar Assad said Monday in his first public comments since fleeing to Moscow that he wanted to keep fighting in Syria. In a statement posted to an official Telegram channel, Assad said Russia requested he leave Syria.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: EU seeks assurances from Syria's new leaders in exchange for dropping sanctions
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union nations on Monday set out conditions for lifting sanctions on Syria and kick-starting aid to the conflict-ravaged country amid uncertainty about its new leaders' intentions just over a week after they seized power.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine and US say some North Korean troops have been killed fighting alongside Russian forces
item.description: Ukraine’s military intelligence agency and the Pentagon say some North Korean troops have been killed during combat against Ukrainian forces in Russia’s Kursk border region.
item.dc:creator: Samya Kullab, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Death toll in Gaza from the Israel-Hamas war tops 45,000 Palestinians, health officials say
item.description: Health officials in the Gaza Strip say more than 45,000 Palestinians have now been killed in the war between Israel and Hamas militants.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Samy Magdy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: For the first time in 8 years, a US Navy ship makes a port call in Cambodia, a top Chinese ally
item.description: SIHANOUKVILLE, Cambodia (AP) — A U.S. Navy warship arrived Monday in Cambodia, the first such visit in eight years to a nation that is China’s close ally in Southeast Asia. Cambodia's government has suggested the port call reflects an upgrade in often-strained relations.
item.dc:creator: SOPHENG CHEANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian paramilitary soldiers killed in friendly fire attack by North Koreans after enlisting DPRK help
item.description: North Korean troops recently killed Russian soldiers in a friendly fire attack, according to Ukrainian intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel will close its Ireland embassy over Gaza tensions as Palestinian death toll nears 45,000
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel said Sunday it will close its embassy in Ireland as relations deteriorated over the war in Gaza, where Palestinian medical officials said new Israeli airstrikes killed over 46 people including several children.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli hostages' families hold Central Park rally, call on Biden, Trump to bring loved ones home
item.description: Family members of Israeli hostages met in New York City on Sunday to call for President Biden and President-elect Trump to bring them home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: West Africa regional bloc approves exit timeline for 3 coup-hit member states
item.description: ABUJA, Nigeria (AP) — West Africa’s regional bloc ECOWAS approved Sunday an exit timeline for three coup-hit nations after a nearly yearlong process of mediation to avert the unprecedented disintegration of the grouping.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazilian President Lula discharged from hospital after surgery to stop brain bleed
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva was discharged from the hospital Sunday after undergoing surgery to stop a brain bleed.
item.dc:creator: THIAGO MOSTAZO and MAYCRON ABADE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Chronicling the Assad regime from death of the father to defeat of the son
item.description: Fox News senior foreign affairs correspondent Greg Palkot documents his years of reporting from Syria in the aftermath of the fall of the regime of Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Major international figures who died in 2024
item.description: Numerous key international figures died in 2024, ranging from Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny to Yahya Sinwar, the leader of Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US reiterates support for South Korea after impeachment of president
item.description: The United States reiterated its support for South Korea, an ally in East Asia, and vowed to advance mutual interests after the country’s president Yoon Suk Yeol was impeached by the parliament.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Pelosi 'on the mend' after hip replacement surgery in Germany
item.description: Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.), who was hospitalized during a trip to Luxembourg, underwent hip surgery early Saturday, according to a spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, The Hill

item.title: Blinken confirms US contact with Syrian rebel group
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken said that U.S. officials have been in "direct contact" with Hayat Tahrir al-Sham (HTS), the Syrian opposition group that effectively led to the collapse of Bashar Assad's regime in the country, the Associated Press reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: South Korea’s president impeached over brief martial law decree
item.description: South Korea's parliament voted to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol early Saturday morning, after a brief decree of martial law thrust the country into temporary political peril. The motion to impeach Yoon passed with a 204-85 vote, The Associated Press (AP) reported.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: South Korea lawmakers vote to impeach president over martial law declaration
item.description: South Korean lawmakers on Saturday voted to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his short-lived martial law declaration earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese phone hack victims haven't been notified: Report
item.description: AT&T and Verizon have reportedly not informed some hacking victims that they were specifically targeted.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Syrians celebrate Assad's fall as US seeks a peaceful political transition
item.description: Thousands of Syrians gathered in Damascus’ main square and a historic mosque for the first Muslim Friday prayers since former President Bashar Assad was overthrown, a major symbolic moment for the country’s dramatic change of power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iran has new drone ship in Persian Gulf
item.description: Iran has a new ship with the capability of launching drones, according to satellite images reviewed by Maxar Technologies that were shared with The Hill. The new drone ship, called Shahid Bagheri, is anchored offshore of the Iranian naval port of Bandar Abbas in the Persian Gulf, according to Maxar.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu vows to challenge 'absurd' corruption charges as he takes the stand in his trial
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu promised defiantly on Tuesday to knock down corruption allegations against him as he began testifying in his long-running trial, becoming the first sitting Israeli leader to take the stand as a criminal defendant.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 8 killed in building collapse in Cairo
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — An apartment building collapsed on Tuesday in Egypt’s capital, killing at least eight people, authorities said. The Health Ministry said in a statement that the collapse of the six-story building in Cairo’s western neighborhood of Waili also injured three people, who were hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
